etymology.description: semantic 衣(衣) (yī, "clothes") + phonetic 壯(壯) (zhuàng) -> clothing-related action or concept
etymology.interpretation: The combination of 衣(衣) (yī, "clothes") as the semantic part suggests a connection to clothing or dress, while 壯(壯) (zhuàng) provides the pronunciation. The core idea of 'clothing' extends metaphorically to 'pretending' or 'acting as,' similar to dressing up or adopting a role.
etymology.simplification: 装(裝): The traditional character 裝 was simplified by removing the semantic 衣(衣) component, streamlining the character for easier writing.

examples.chinese: 他装得很开心(他裝得很開心)。
examples.english: He pretended to be very happy.

examples.chinese: 我不想装不知道(我不想裝不知道)，因此直接问了他(因此直接問了他)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ bù xiǎng zhuāng bù zhīdào, yīncǐ zhíjiē wèn le tā.
examples.english: I didn't want to pretend not to know, so I asked him directly.

examples.chinese: 有人喜欢在公众场合装作别人(有人喜歡在公衆場合裝作別人)。
examples.pinyin: Yǒurén xǐhuān zài gōngzhòng chǎnghé zhuāng zuò biérén.
examples.english: Some people like to pretend to be someone else in public places.
